multiplechoiceresponse.label: A patient differs from an otherwise identical patient in exactly one way: they have oldpeak that is higher. According to the model, which statement is correct?

choicegroup.choice: The patient receives higher predicted heart disease risk.
The patient receives lower predicted heart disease risk.
The model cannot use numeric variables to adjust risk.
The effect of oldpeak depends entirely on categorical variables.

div.p: oldpeak has a negative coefficient, which means that as its value increases, the model decreases the predicted log-odds of heart disease, holding all other features fixed. This interpretation applies regardless of the patient’s categorical indicators. The statement concerns model behavior only, not clinical meaning.
